title: getOwnerRepo() mishandles scp-style SSH URLs with a custom port
body: getOwnerRepo() in source-parser.ts matches `git@host:owner/repo.git` and `ssh://git@host:port/owner/repo.git`, but a bare scp-style URL with an embedded port like `git@host:7999:owner/repo.git` (used by some self-hosted GitLab/Bitbucket setups) doesn't match either regex and getOwnerRepo returns null, breaking lockfile tracking for those sources.

title: Support pinning `skills add` to a specific commit SHA
body: `npx skills add <package>` seems to resolve to the default branch or a tag, but there's no documented way to pin to an exact commit SHA for reproducible installs across a team. Could add.ts accept something like `owner/repo@sha:<hash>`?

title: detect-agent picks the wrong agent when multiple config dirs exist
body: On a machine with both a Claude Code config dir and a Cursor config dir present, detect-agent.ts seems to just return whichever it checks first rather than the one actually invoking the `skills` CLI (e.g. via $AGENT env var or process ancestry). This causes `skills install` to write to the wrong provider directory.

title: Confusing naming between scripts/sync-agents.ts and src/sync.ts
body: It took a while to figure out that `scripts/sync-agents.ts` is a maintainer-only script for syncing the supported agent registry, while `src/sync.ts` is the actual runtime command behind `skills sync` for end users. Could the docs (or a README note) clarify the distinction so contributors don't confuse the two?

title: Symlink-based install fails on Windows without Developer Mode/admin
body: installer.ts appears to default to symlinking skill files into the target agent directory, which throws EPERM on Windows unless Developer Mode or an elevated shell is used. Given `skills` targets multiple agent CLIs, should the installer detect this and fall back to copying automatically instead of erroring out?
